interactions.evidence_quote: The results shows significant decrease in CL & k 10 , and significant elevation in t 1/2 , AUC 0- & V ss of PAH in test group compared to control group clearly indicates the cumulative inhibitory effect of phenolic acids present in the AREDH. Further, the co-administration of AREDH with any hOAT1 substrate, results in decrease in clearance and increase in the bioavailability and half-life of the substrate drugs.

title: THE NEED FOR HERBOVIGILANCE

title: A role for physicians in ethnopharmacology and drug discovery.

title: Lack of pharmacokinetic interaction between St. John's wort and prednisone.

interactions.evidence_quote: Twenty-eight days of SJW treatment resulted in no significant alterations in the pharmacokinetic parameters for prednisone or prednisolone. Oral administration of prednisone resulted in prednisone mean +/- SD area under the curves (AUCs) of 115.89 +/- 39.52 microg x h/L prior to SJW treatment and 128.76 +/- 32.71 microg x h/L after 28 days of treatment.

Prednisolone mean AUCs were 714.19 +/- 153.29 microg x h/L before SJW administration and 700.74 +/- 89.68 microg x h/L after treatment. Concurrent administration of SJW had no significant effect on the single-dose pharmacokinetics of prednisone or metabolic prednisolone in male subjects.

extraction.extraction_notes: The study specifically tested St. John's wort coadministration with prednisone and its metabolite prednisolone and found no significant pharmacokinetic interaction.

title: Tanshinone IIA increases the bystander effect of herpes simplex virus thymidine kinase/ganciclovir gene therapy via enha

interactions.specific_target: gap junctional intercellular communication; connexins Cx26 and Cx43
interactions.evidence_quote: Tan IIA increased GJIC in B16 melanoma cells, leading to more efficient GCV-induced bystander killing in cells stably expressing HSV-tk. Additionally, in vivo experiments demonstrated that tumors in mice with 10% HSV-tk positive B16 cells and 90% wild-type B16 cells became smaller following treatment with the combination of GCV and Tan IIA as compared to GCV or Tan IIA alone.

extraction.extraction_notes: Tanshinone IIA is described as a chemical substance derived from a Chinese medicine herb; the source herb is not named in the abstract. Interaction is pharmacodynamic enhancement of HSV-tk/ganciclovir suicide gene therapy via increased gap junction coupling.

title: Effects of berberine on the pharmacokinetics of losartan and its metabolite EXP3174 in rats and its mechanism.

interactions.evidence_quote: The Cmax (1.26 ± 0.37 versus 1.96 ± 0.45 mg/L) and the AUC(0-t) (8.25 ± 0.89 versus 12.70 ± 1.42 mg h/L) of losartan were significantly (p < 0.05) increased by BBR compared to the control... We infer that BBR might increase the plasma concentration of losartan and decrease the concentration of EXP3174 through inhibiting the activity of CYP3A4 or CYP2C9.

The Cmax (0.97 ± 0.15 versus 0.77 ± 0.06 mg/L) of EXP3174 was significantly decreased compared to the control (p < 0.05)... We infer that BBR might increase the plasma concentration of losartan and decrease the concentration of EXP3174 through inhibiting the activity of CYP3A4 or CYP2C9.

extraction.extraction_notes: Rat pharmacokinetic study with supporting rat liver microsome metabolic stability data; sample size was not stated in the abstract.

title: Interaction of green tea polyphenol epigallocatechin-3-gallate with sunitinib: Potential risk of diminished sunitinib bi

interactions.specific_target: precipitation/physical interaction in gastrointestinal contents reducing oral bioavailability
interactions.evidence_quote: The AUC0-∞ and C max of plasma sunitinib were markedly reduced by co-administration of EGCG to rats. Our study firstly showed that EGCG interacted with sunitinib and reduced the bioavailability of sunitinib.

extraction.extraction_notes: The abstract also reports an mRCC patient in whom tea drinking appeared to interfere with symptom control during sunitinib therapy, but quantitative evidence comes from in vitro interaction/precipitation studies and rat pharmacokinetic experiments.

title: Effect of pomegranate juice on intestinal transport and pharmacokinetics of nitrendipine in rats

interactions.evidence_quote: There was significant increase in effective permeability, absorption rate constant and fraction of drug absorbed in the pretreated group when compared with the control group, probably due to inhibition of the P-glycoprotein- mediated efflux of the drug by PJ.

In comparison with control, PJ treatment significantly increased the are under the concentration-time curve of oral nitrendipine. The peak plasm concentration of nitrendipine was also significantly increased by PJ. However, elimination half-life of nitrendipine was not altered significantly in both PJ co-administered and pretreated groups. These results suggest that PJ inhibits the intestinal metabolism of nitrendipine without inhibiting the hepatic metabolism in rats.

extraction.extraction_notes: Rat pharmacokinetic and intestinal perfusion study; abstract reports significant AUC and Cmax increases but does not provide numerical percent changes or sample size.

title: Initial dose optimization of tacrolimus for children with systemic lupus erythematosus based on the CYP3A5 polymorphism

interactions.evidence_quote: The results showed that weight, the CYP3A5 genotype and combined treatment with Wuzhi capsule can affect tacrolimus clearance in children with systemic lupus erythematosus. Furthermore, at the same weight, the ratios of tacrolimus clearance were 1:2.49:0.57:1.4193 from people who were CYP3A5*3/*3 and did not take Wuzhi capsule, people who had the CYP3A5*1 allele and did not take Wuzhi capsule, people who were CYP3A5*3/*3 and took Wuzhi capsule, and people who had the CYP3A5*1 allele and took Wuzhi capsule, respectively.

extraction.extraction_notes: Wuzhi capsule coadministration was associated with lower tacrolimus clearance. Relative to genotype-matched patients not taking Wuzhi capsule, clearance was approximately 43% lower in both CYP3A5*3/*3 and CYP3A5*1 groups, implying increased tacrolimus exposure; AUC was not directly reported.

title: Effects of Herba Erigerontis injection on pharmacodynamics and pharmacokinetics of warfarin in rats in vivo

interactions.evidence_quote: Pharmacokinetic study manifested that Cmax, AUC and t1/2 prolonged significantly (P <.01) for R/S-warfarin in presence of HEI. Low (3.6 mL/kg), medium (7.2 mL/kg) and high (10.8 mL/kg) doses of HEI could decrease plasma protein binding rate of warfarin significantly (P <.01).

Pharmacodynamic study demonstrated that both HEI alone and co-administered with warfarin could increase PT and INR values significantly (P <.01), whereas the APTT values were unaffected (P >.05). The results mean that HEI can potentiate the anticoagulant response of warfarin through both pharmacodynamics and pharmacokinetics.

interactions.evidence_quote: Further analysis of other crude extracts demonstrated potent inhibition of CYP1A2 activity for a dried plant ethanol extract (1.5 mu g/mL), fresh plant ethanol extract (3.9 mu g/mL), and moderate activity for a fresh plant aqueous extract (27.8 mu g/mL). ... Individual screening of these phytochemicals against the activity of CYP1A2 identified yatein as a moderate inhibitor (71.9 mu M), likely to contribute to the plant extract's potent bioactivity.
The dried plant aqueous extract demonstrated potent inhibition of the activities of CYPs 1A1 (7.6 mu g/mL), 1A2 (1.9 mu g/mL), 1B1 (9.4 mu g/mL) and 3A4 (6.8 mu g/mL).
The dried plant aqueous extract demonstrated potent inhibition of the activities of CYPs 1A1 (7.6 mu g/mL), 1A2 (1.9 mu g/mL), 1B1 (9.4 mu g/mL) and 3A4 (6.8 mu g/mL).
The dried plant aqueous extract demonstrated potent inhibition of the activities of CYPs 1A1 (7.6 mu g/mL), 1A2 (1.9 mu g/mL), 1B1 (9.4 mu g/mL) and 3A4 (6.8 mu g/mL).

extraction.extraction_notes: In vitro CYP inhibition by Hyptis verticillata extracts implies potential pharmacokinetic interactions with drugs that are substrates of the inhibited CYP enzymes; no specific conventional drug was tested in the abstract.
